event.summary: Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.

acceptance.proof: Three explicit caller-supplied lanes admit each of the eleven accepted O roles exactly once before producing StreamInfoVolatileFields.
Oversized lanes, cross-lane roles, same-lane duplicates, and the first missing fixed-order role reject with typed stable evidence before O delegation.
Accepted strings move once into O without parsing or reallocation; address and port roles remain opaque and O retains text-bound authority.
The contract is one-shot snapshot admission only and explicitly makes no stale/current inference because accepted evidence supplies no clock, revision, epoch, or freshness witness.
Dependencies, features, unsafe code, platform acquisition, runtime effects, devices, and authority remain absent; public and workflow contracts pass.

document.commit_policy: Commit the pure caller-supplied snapshot admission contract separately from O representation, R document spelling, actual acquisition, adapters, transport, runtime activation, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the provider-input snapshot admission lifecycle, disjoint lanes, rejection order, and freshness boundary.
Describe the bounded snapshot admission contract without promoting it to acquisition or provider runtime behavior.
Review reusable authority and lifecycle guidance; retain unchanged because current evidence and adapter boundaries already require honest freshness claims.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review repo-family routing; retain unchanged because the public LSL and Manifold boundaries remain intact.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review installed skill without public mutation.

non_scope: Any modification to accepted O or R source or evidence, Cargo manifests or locks, project or feature locks, oracle drivers, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, native libraries, or implementation-source inputs.
Clock, revision, epoch, freshness, currentness, expiry, environment, host, randomness, socket, network, address, port, protocol, or wire semantics.

Provider selection or execution, implementation/runtime/transport acquisition adapters, OS or platform inspection, discovery, inlet, outlet, transport, buffering, recovery, FFI, runtime activation, device, package, or permission behavior.
XML construction, representation, parsing, serialization, document spelling, or modification of LSLC-001R.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, routing, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, or product-policy authority.
document.objective: Consume three explicit disjoint caller-supplied implementation-assigned, runtime-assigned, and transport-owned value lanes; fail closed unless every accepted LSLC-001O role appears exactly once in its owning lane; then move the complete opaque snapshot into StreamInfoVolatileFields without acquisition, freshness inference, XML/document behavior, transport semantics, runtime activation, device effects, or Manifold authority.
outputs: A dependency-free one-shot snapshot candidate with three explicit ownership lanes and allocation-preserving role/value entries.
Deterministic typed rejection for lane bounds, cross-lane roles, duplicate roles, first missing fixed-order role, and unchanged delegated O text-bound errors.
An accepted snapshot wrapper exposing borrowed and consuming access to the produced StreamInfoVolatileFields only after complete internally consistent admission.
Focused positive and damaged evidence proving that no stale/current claim is possible without separately accepted freshness evidence.
